name: Chamomile
summary: Chamomile may support mild anxiety or sleep quality, especially as evening tea or standardized extract.
description: Gentle sleep/stress support with modest human evidence and traditional use.
safetyNotes: Generally well tolerated for most users.

sources.title: Warfarin and food, herbal or dietary supplement interactions: A systematic review
Anti-Inflammatory and Skin Barrier Repair Effects of Topical Application of Some Plant Oils
Chamomile: A Review of Its Traditional Uses, Chemical Constituents, Pharmacological Activities and Quality Control Studies
The Effect of Oral Chamomile on Anxiety: A Systematic Review of Clinical Trials
sources.authors: Sogand Saadatmand; Foad Zohroudi; Hadith Tangestani

sources.title: Effects of chamomile (Matricaria chamomilla L.) on sleep: A systematic review and meta-analysis of clinical trials
sources.authors: Asma Kazemi; S. Shojaei-Zarghani; Parham Eskandarzadeh; Mohammad Hashem Hashempur
sources.note: chamomile sleep quality evidence and limitation flags

claimMap.claim: Oral chamomile may reduce anxiety symptoms, but evidence needs stronger standardization.
claimMap.notes: Oral chamomile may reduce anxiety symptoms, but evidence needs stronger standardization.

qualifiers.population: Clinical trial participants with anxiety symptoms

claimMap.claim: Chamomile may improve sleep quality, especially subjective PSQI scores and nighttime awakenings, but evidence has heterogeneity and limited objective data.
claimMap.notes: Chamomile may improve sleep quality, especially subjective PSQI scores and nighttime awakenings, but evidence has heterogeneity and limited objective data.
